title: DYNAMIC CHANNEL ALLOCATION OF NARROWBAND INTERNET OF THINGS AND LONG-TERM EVOLUTION MACHINE COMMUNICATIONS
summary: Aspects provided herein provide methods, systems, and a non-transitory computer storage media storing computer-useable instructions for dynamic allocation of internet of things (IoT) device communications. The method begins with determining an amount of unallocated spectrum in a communications channel used by one or more devices attached to a node. Next, it is determined that the IoT device has requested an amount of spectrum from the node that is less than the amount of unallocated spectrum. At least a portion of the amount of unallocated spectrum is dynamically allocated to the IoT device.
details:
AMENDMENTS TO THE CLAIMS:

 This listing of claims will replace all prior versions, and listings, of claims in the application:

 Listing of Claims:

 1

 1. (Currently Amended) A method of dynamic channel allocation for an internet of things (IoT) device, the method comprising:

 monitoring activity of one or more devices wirelessly communicating with a node;

 determining, based on the monitored activity, an amount of unallocated spectrum in a communications channel used by the node to wirelessly communicate with the one or more devices;

 determining for a first device of the one or more devices, a bandwidth requirement associated with a first communication protocol used by the first device;

 determining that the amount of unallocated spectrum is less than the bandwidth requirement associated with the first communication protocol;

 comparing the amount of unallocated spectrum to the bandwidth requirement associated with the communication protocol; and

 based on the comparisondetermination, dynamically allocating at least a portion of the unallocated spectrum to a second device of the one or more devices, wherein the second device uses a second communication protocol that is different from the first communication protocol used by the first device.

 Currently amended

 2

 2. (Original) The method of claim 1
, wherein the first protocol uses a greater amount of the unallocated spectrum than the second protocol.

 Original

 3

 3. (Original) The method of claim 1
, wherein the second protocol comprises narrowband internet of things (NB-IoT).

 Original

 4

 4. (Original) The method of claim 3
, wherein the first protocol comprises long term evolution category machine one (LTE CAT M1).

 Original

 5

 5. (Original) The method of claim 4
, wherein the first portion comprises one physical resource block and the second portion comprises six physical resource blocks.

 Original

 6

 6. (Currently Amended) A system for performing dynamic channel allocation, the system comprising:

 a wireless access point having one or more radios and one or more antennas configured to wirelessly communicate with one or more wireless devices; and

 one or more computer processing components configured to perform operations for dynamic IoT scheduling, the operations comprising:

 monitoring activity of a plurality of devices communicating with the wireless access point;

 determining, based on the monitored activity, an amount of unallocated bandwidth of a channel;

 determining for a first device of the plurality of devices, a bandwidth requirement associated with a first communication protocol used for the first device;

 determining that the amount of unallocated spectrum is less than the bandwidth requirement associated with the first communication protocol;

 comparing the amount of unallocated bandwidth to the bandwidth requirement; and

 based on the comparisondetermining, allocating at least a portion of the unallocated bandwidth to a second device of the plurality of devices,wherein the second device uses a second communication protocol different from the first communication protocol used by the first device.

 Currently amended

 7

 7. (Original) The system of claim 6
, wherein the first protocol is Wi-Fi.

 Original

 8

 8. (Currently Amended) The system of claim 7
, wherein the wireless access point is a local area network node, and wherein each of the first set of device[[s]] and the second set of device is wirelessly connected to the local area network node.

 Currently amended

 9

 9. (Original) The system of claim 8
, wherein the channel is unlicensed spectrum.

 Original

 10

 10. (Currently Amended) The system of claim 9
, wherein the operations further comprise receiving an indication an IoT device of the second set of one or more device of the plurality of devices is requesting a data stream.

 Currently amended

 11

 11. (Original) The system of claim 10
, wherein allocating at least the portion of the second amount of bandwidth comprises allocating an amount of bandwidth to the IoT device, the size of the data stream being less than the second amount of bandwidth.

 Original

 12

 12. (Original) The system of claim 11
, wherein the second protocol is narrowband internet of things (NB-IoT).

 Original

 13

 13. (Original) The system of claim 11
, wherein the second protocol is a long term evolution category machine one (LTE CAT M1).

 Original

 14

 14. (Currently Amended) A non-transitory computer storage media storing computer-useable instructions that, when used by one or more processors, cause the processors to:

 monitor activity of a plurality of devices wirelessly communicating with a wireless access point;

 determine, based on the monitored activity, an amount of unallocated bandwidth of a channel;

 determine, for a first device of the plurality of devices, a bandwidth requirement associated with a first communication protocol used for the first device;

 determine that the amount of unallocated bandwidth is less than the bandwidth requirement;

 compare the amount of unallocated bandwidth to the bandwidth requirement; and

 based on the comparisondetermination, allocate a second portion of the unallocated bandwidth to a second device of the plurality of devices, wherein the second device[[s]] utilizes a second communication protocol to wirelessly communicate with the wireless access point that is different from the first communication protocol used by the first device.

 Currently amended

 15

 15. (Currently Amended) The non-transitory computer storage media of claim 14
, wherein the wireless access point is a local area network node, and wherein each of the first the first set of device[[s]] and the second set of device is wirelessly connected to the local area network node.

 Currently amended

 16

 16. (Currently Amended) The non-transitory computer storage media of claim 15
, wherein the operations further comprise receiving an indication an IoT device of the second set of one or more device of the plurality of devices is requesting a data stream.

 Currently amended

 17

 17. (Original) The non-transitory computer storage media of claim 16
, wherein allocating at least the portion of the second amount of bandwidth comprises allocating an amount of bandwidth to the IoT device, the size of the data stream being less than the second amount of bandwidth.

 Original

 18

 18. (Currently Amended) The non-transitory computer storage media of claim 17
, wherein the first protocol uses a greater amount of the unallocated spectrumbandwidth than the second protocol.

 Currently amended

 19

 19. (Currently Amended) The non-transitory computer storage media of claim 18
, wherein the second communication protocol is narrowband internet of things (NB-IoT).

 Currently amended

 20. (Currently Amended) The non-transitory computer storage media of claim 19
, wherein the second communication protocol is a long term evolution category machine one (LTE CAT M1).

 Currently amended
